condition.selected: I picked the line closest to the top of the bar

incorrects.description: Student selected the closest-line strategy (doesn't account for between-line precision)

params.description: Bar remains highlighted. Ghost gridlines at 5s pulse briefly.

steps.text: That gets you close, but the bar is exactly halfway between 40 and 50. What number is halfway?

condition.selected: I added the two numbers on the lines

incorrects.description: Student selected adding the gridline numbers (arithmetic error—confuses sum with midpoint)

params.description: Bar remains highlighted. Ghost gridlines at 5s pulse briefly.

steps.text: Adding 40 and 50 gives 90, which is too big. To find halfway, you need half of the difference. What's half of 10?

incorrects.description: Student selected guessing (lacks awareness of the halfway pattern strategy)

params.description: Bar remains highlighted. Ghost gridlines at 5s pulse briefly.

steps.text: You don't need to guess. There's a pattern: halfway between any two lines is always 5 more than the lower line. Why is that?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct strategy.

params.description: Ghost gridlines at 5s pulse. The space between 40 and 50 highlights briefly, then the halfway point at 45 glows.

steps.text: Let me show you the pattern. The bar is halfway between 40 and 50. The lines are 10 apart. Half of 10 is 5. So halfway is always 5 more than the lower line: 40 plus 5 equals 45. That pattern works for any bar graph with scale of 10. Halfway between the lines is always 5 more.
